contradictions.claim: Conscious Choice: verified sustainable product lines
contradictions.evidence_text: Norway's Forbrukertilsynet banned H&M's 'Conscious' labeling as misleading, finding the sustainability index used to qualify products was not scientifically valid and that claims were unsubstantiated. H&M withdrew 96 product claims in Norway.

As of FY2023, 82.5% of H&M's materials are defined as 'more sustainable', however H&M Group's own definition of 'more sustainable' includes materials like conventional Better Cotton that are disputed by independent environmental groups.

contradictions.claim: Fair wages for all garment workers in supply chain
contradictions.evidence_text: H&M garment workers in Bangladesh earning $95/month, requiring 3x their wages to reach living wage standards. H&M has not published a living wage roadmap or timeline.

contradictions.evidence_source: Norwegian Consumer Authority Ruling + Current DE Page
contradictions.evidence_text: H&M was found guilty of misleading sustainability claims in Norway in 2022. The German page continues to use nearly identical 'Conscious' labeling and claims without the disclosures or modifications required by Norwegian regulators.

contradictions.evidence_source: H&M SEC Filing (20-F equivalent), Nasdaq Stockholm 2023
contradictions.evidence_text: H&M's 2040 climate positive target is described as an 'ambition' contingent on 'industry-wide systemic change' and 'policy support'. Interim 2025 and 2030 targets are not legally binding and may be revised.

sec_filing.sec_language: H&M Group defines 'more sustainable materials' according to its own Sustainability Index. External verification of this definition is limited. Achievement of the 2030 target is contingent on supplier compliance and industry-wide availability of certified materials.
sec_filing.delta_description: Public claims state unqualified '100% sustainably sourced' commitment; regulatory filing reveals the definition of 'sustainable' is self-defined, not externally verified, and the target has no enforcement mechanism.

news_violations.headline: H&M's 'Conscious' labeling banned in Norway as misleading
news_violations.summary: Forbrukertilsynet ruled H&M's Higg Index-based sustainability scoring was not scientifically valid and banned 96 product claims as greenwashing

news_violations.headline: H&M faces class action over 'false' green advertising in US
news_violations.summary: New York federal court class action alleges H&M's sustainability scorecards were misleading and violated consumer protection laws

news_violations.headline: UK ASA investigation into H&M sustainability claims launched
news_violations.summary: ASA opens formal investigation following complaints that H&M's Move to a Climate Positive Fashion Future campaign made unsubstantiated claims

sample_reviews: The 'Conscious' collection is 90% marketing. No one internally believes we're anywhere near our sustainability targets.
After Norway banned our claims, we changed nothing except removing Norway-specific language from some materials.
Sustainability targets get set at board level for investor relations. No one on the ground has a roadmap to hit them.
The garment workers thing is a real problem and we all know it. The public messaging does not reflect reality.
